primary_concern: Sepsis with concurrent acute kidney injury (AKI) and coagulopathy
clinical_summary: Elevated lactate (4.2 mmol/L) combined with leukocytosis (14.2 K/uL) and thrombocytopenia (88-95 K/uL) meet Sepsis-3 criteria for sepsis risk. AKI is evident with staged progression from Stage 1 (Cr 2.1 mg/dL) to Stage 3 (Cr 8.9 mg/dL). Thrombocytopenia (<100 K/uL) meets SOFA coagulation scoring criteria. Bilirubin elevation (1.4 mg/dL) and anemia (Hgb 9.8 g/dL) suggest possible hepatic or hemolytic involvement.

prioritized_risks.explanation: Lactate >2 mmol/L, WBC >12 K/uL, and creatinine elevation meet Sepsis-3 criteria (suspected infection + SOFA increase ≥2)
Creatinine rise ≥1.5× baseline (from 2.1 to 8.9 mg/dL) meets KDIGO Stage 3 AKI criteria
Platelets <100 K/uL meets SOFA coagulation score ≥2 (threshold: <150 K/uL for score ≥1)
Lactate >2 mmol/L present but no documentation of vasopressor-dependent hypotension
prioritized_risks.threshold: Vasopressor-dependent MAP ≥65 mmHg + lactate >2 mmol/L post-fluids

recommended_actions: Initiate broad-spectrum antibiotics per sepsis protocol
Order urgent source control evaluation for suspected infection
Begin renal replacement therapy consultation for Stage 3 AKI
Monitor platelet trends and consider transfusion threshold assessment
Recheck creatinine with confirmatory testing to validate rapid rise
doctor_handoff: Review creatinine outlier (8.9 mg/dL) for specimen validity, assess for hypotension requiring vasopressors, and prioritize infection source identification. Monitor hourly urine output and lactate trends.

disease_progression.observation: Baseline abnormalities: WBC 14.2 K/uL, Hgb 9.8 g/dL, Bilirubin 1.4 mg/dL
AKI Stage 3 (Cr 8.9 mg/dL) with persistent thrombocytopenia (88 K/uL)

excluded_outliers.reason: Rapid change detected — Creatinine changed by 6.5 mg/dL within 6.0h (max allowed: 2.0). Probable measurement error or specimen issue. Section 15.2 — rise > 2.0 mg/dL in < 6h.
